design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse side-scrolling levels by jumping between platforms, defeating enemies via jumping/rolling/throwing, collecting items, and avoiding obstacles. Swap between two Kongs (Donkey and Diddy) with different abilities to overcome level challenges.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing two distinct Kong abilities (Donkey's strength vs Diddy's speed/agility) to navigate hazards. One Kong being hit forces control switch, creating resource tension around Kong availability.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as iconic ape characters recovering stolen banana hoard from Kremling army through acrobatic platforming. The pre-rendered visuals sell a tactile, weighty character-driven adventure.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits to asymmetric co-character design philosophy: Donkey and Diddy are meaningfully different, not cosmetic variants. Levels are designed around swapping between them. This contrasts with many platformers offering identical alternate characters. The game also champions pre-rendered 3D-to-2D sprite conversion as its artistic identity, making visual polish a core design pillar.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple hit tolerance per Kong before losing control; hitting obstacles causes active Kong to flee and auto-switches to partner Kong. Game continues until both Kongs are lost.
difficulty.continues_system: Implicit continue system via life pool management across levels; world map structure allows level retry without hard restart.
difficulty.checkpointing: Levels appear segmented with implicit checkpoints based on level structure (barrels, minecarts, and boss arenas serve as natural progression gates); respawn after hitting obstacles restores to recent safe state via Kong swap mechanic.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players choose which Kong to use for platforming challenges. Donkey Kong better for defeating enemies directly; Diddy Kong better for precise acrobatic navigation. Secret bonus stages and 101% completion represent optional difficulty extension.

progression.upgrade_system: No persistent upgrades between levels. Kong selection and temporary animal companions (granting special abilities like Yoshi-analogs) provide level-specific capability variation.
progression.unlock_mechanism: 40 sequential levels accessed via world map. Levels unlock linearly with progression; boss completion gates access to subsequent areas.
replayability_hooks: Secret bonus stages hidden throughout levels encourage revisit and exploration
101% completion challenge incentivizes collecting all items and finding hidden secrets
Two-player cooperative and racing modes extend replayability
Different Kong swap strategies create alternate routes through same level geometry

world_building_techniques: Level theming (jungles, underwater reefs, caves, mines, mountains, factories) establishes geography and Kremling territory occupation
Boss encounters at area ends punctuate story beats without explicit cutscenes
Character differentiation (Donkey Kong vs Diddy Kong) embedded in mechanical asymmetry communicates their relationship
Banana hoard as MacGuffin drives motivation through level progression

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as area culminations, mechanically distinct from standard level hazards. Each area's final challenge represents escalation of environment-specific threats (creatures native to that biome/factory). Bosses test accumulated Kong abilities and character selection strategy.
notable_bosses: King K. Rool (implied final boss based on narrative setup)

faction_indicators: Kremling army as antagonist faction occupying level environments
Kong family (Donkey Kong and Diddy Kong) as protagonists reclaiming territory
Animal companions as neutral/allied forces providing temporary power
danger_signals: Kremling enemy placement density increases toward boss arenas
Obstacle complexity escalation (minecarts, cannons, rope mechanics) signals progression into more dangerous territory
Boss arena transitions mark entry into high-stakes encounters
environmental_storytelling: Jungle levels show pristine Kong habitat before Kremling occupation
Mine and factory levels indicate industrial Kremling infrastructure and resource extraction
Level names and themes establish geographic progression through Kremling-controlled islands
Animal companion presence suggests symbiotic ecosystem relationships Kongs leverage
